title: Quality assurance and regulatory professionals
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of a recreational facility, including cruise ships and parks. All personal service managers not listed separately. Manage or coordinate fitness and wellness programs and services. Manage and train staff of wellness specialists, health educators, or fitness instructors. (Based on 56 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Conduct internet-based and library research, and perform data entry and other clerical work as required for project completion. Compile statistics from source materials, compute and analyze data using statistical formulas, prepare, manipulate, and manage extensive databases, and enter data into computers for use in analyses or reports. Geneate reports, charts, or visualizations that describe and interpret the findings of analyses.

Design surveys, opinion polls, or other instruments to collect data and provide assistance in the design of survey instruments such as questionnaires. Recruit and schedule research participants, track research participants, and perform any necessary follow-up tasks. Administer standardized tests to research subjects or interview them to collect research data.

Perform quality analyses of data inputs and resulting analyses or predictions, and conduct quality control audits to ensure accuracy, completeness, or proper usage of clinical systems and data. Check source data to verify completeness and accuracy, and verify the accuracy and validity of data entered in databases, correcting any errors as necessary.

Develop and implement research quality control procedures and assist with the preparation of project-related reports, manuscripts, and presentations. Participate in the publication of data or information, contribute to the compilation and production of protocols, clinical study reports, and regulatory submissions.

Train staff or researchers on the use of databases and technical procedures or software program usage. Provide technical support for existing reports, dashboards, or other tools, and identify and analyze industry or geographic trends with business strategy implications. Track the flow of work forms, including in-house data flow or electronic forms transfer to ensure compliance with standard operating procedures.

Check survey responses for errors, select statistical tests for analyzing data, and monitor work productivity or quality. Confer with clients or end users about project timelines and changes and discuss data presentation requirements with them.

Code data in preparation for computer entry and track laboratory supplies and expenses. Organize paperwork for distribution or analysis, perform routine system administrative functions, and file data and related information while maintaining and updating databases.

Identify relationships and trends, analyze or manipulate data, create or review technical design documentation, and document all changes to databases. Analyze technology trends, interpret logistics data, and maintain awareness of new and emerging computational methods and technologies to support recommendations for action.

Generate standard or custom reports summarizing business, financial, or economic data for review, manage timely flow of business intelligence information to users, and ensure that pricing structures reflect logistics costing accurately. Prepare logistic strategies or conceptual designs for production facilities and propose logistics solutions for customers.

Analyze competitive market strategies and evaluate the use of technologies to improve transportation efficiency, develop logistics metrics, and maintain logistics records in accordance with corporate policies. Identify opportunities for inventory reductions and recommend improvements to existing or planned logistics processes.

Design and implement comprehensive systems and processes to improve project management, including documentation for estimates, training materials, and project plans, while supporting compliance with environmental standards and minimizing costs.

Plan, organize, and execute logistics activities, collaborating with other departments to meet customer requirements and developing proposals for assessment of design alternatives and process improvements.

Design or engineer nanomaterials, nanodevices, or nano-enabled products using computer-aided design (CAD) software and develop processes or test protocols for quality control testing or performance measurement.

Create designs or prototypes for nanosystem applications, conduct tests of new nanotechnology products, and synthesize or characterize nanomaterials to improve their performance or reduce environmental impact.
Provide scientific guidance to engineers and technologists, analyze datasets for research purposes, and develop methodologies for transferring laboratory processes to commercial-scale production.
Prepare technical reports, communicate research findings, and write grant proposals to obtain funding for various research projects in the sciences.
Conduct energy audits, recommend energy savings strategies, and monitor design or construction issues related to energy efficiency and management.
Evaluate product designs for safety and develop industry standards for product safety, including the assessment of health risks from product misuse and compliance with safety regulations.
Manage teams of engineers, develop project plans, and oversee budgets and compliance with regulations on research and development projects.
Inspect facilities or equipment to ensure safety compliance and evaluate the adequacy of safety programs, including worker training regarding safety laws and regulations.
Conduct research to develop methodologies and instrumentation for medical applications, and collaborate with specialists in genetic and biochemical analyses.
Supervise geneticists and technicians, review genetic laboratory results, and prepare findings for publication or presentation at professional conferences.
Maintain laboratory safety programs and train personnel in appropriate safety techniques, as well as evaluate new lab supplies and equipment for operability.

Coordinate molecular or cellular research activities with scientists specializing in other fields, and direct, organize, or prioritize biological laboratory activities while supervising technical personnel and postdoctoral research fellows. Provide scientific direction for project teams regarding the evaluation or handling of devices, drugs, or cells for in vitro and in vivo disease models.
Perform laboratory procedures following protocols including DNA sequencing, cloning and extraction, RNA purification, or gel electrophoresis, and maintain accurate laboratory records and data.
Instruct undergraduate and graduate students in cellular or molecular biology and teach principles of medicine and medical and laboratory procedures to healthcare professionals.

Evaluate new technologies to enhance or complement current research and conduct applied research aimed at improvements in areas such as disease testing, pharmaceuticals, and the harnessing of microbes to recycle waste.

Compile and analyze molecular or cellular experimental data, adjusting experimental designs as necessary, and design molecular or cellular laboratory experiments, overseeing their execution and interpreting results.
Monitor or operate specialized laboratory equipment, ensuring they are maintained in working condition to ensure accuracy and quality of experimental results.
Write grants and papers or attend fundraising events to seek research funds, and prepare reports or recommendations based upon research outcomes.
Prepare technical and research reports, such as environmental impact reports, and communicate the results to public and regulatory agencies.
Conduct research on food safety, including inspection of food processing areas to ensure compliance with government regulations and standards for sanitation, safety, and quality.

Develop new or improved ways of preserving, processing, packaging, storing, and delivering foods based on scientific principles, and evaluate food processing operations to develop quality assurance programs.
Monitor and perform tests on water, food, and the environment to detect harmful microorganisms or to obtain information about sources of pollution, contamination, or infection.
Identify and analyze public health issues related to foodborne parasitic diseases and their impact on public policies, scientific studies, or surveys.

Conduct environmental studies on topics such as nuclear power generation, nuclear waste disposal, or nuclear weapon deployment and develop or contribute to the development of plans to remediate or restore environments affected by nuclear radiation.
Promote awareness or use of alternative or renewable energy sources and analyze, interpret, or create graphical representations of energy data, using engineering software.
Design databases to support research efforts and resolve problems in product development by conferring with process engineers, plant operators, flavor experts, and other specialists.
Develop plans and programs to manage fire safety, including conducting fire risk assessments, advising on fire prevention equipment and techniques, and educating the public about fire safety.
Study the relationships between ignition sources and materials, and determine causes of fires and ways in which they could have been prevented.
Conduct training or in-services to educate personnel on proper use of equipment and evaluate effectiveness of biomedical equipment for safety and efficiency.

Assist in the development of protocols, policies, and standards for the use, maintenance, and repair of medical equipment, while adapting or designing computer hardware or software for medical science uses.
Analyze, fabricate, or test photonic systems or components, and develop or test photonic prototypes or models while ensuring compliance with safety standards in research and operations.
Research chemical effects of substances on biological systems and develop new methods to study mechanisms of biological processes.
Conduct research on cell organization and function, including mechanisms of gene expression, and develop new drugs or medications intended for commercial distribution.
Evaluate safety and effectiveness of various drugs and materials on living cells and organisms while conducting studies on the chemistry of living processes.
Monitor and analyze energy consumption, and advise on energy management techniques while reviewing or negotiating energy purchase agreements.
Educate the public about infectious and communicable diseases, including the significance of disease transmission and prevention methods.
Design or develop environmental monitoring systems or automated control systems related to waste processing, air quality, or water quality systems.
Coordinate preventive programs to control the outbreak of wildlife diseases.
Investigate the nature, composition, or expression of genes while studying physical principles of living cells and organisms and their electrical or mechanical energy.
Design fire detection equipment or alarm systems and consult on safety regulations to recommend improvements as necessary.
Investigate the cause and transmission of diseases, aiding in the development of health safety standards and public health improvement programs.
Design or develop new crystals for photonics applications, but remain engaged with current industry practices and advancements by attending workshops, seminars, or conferences.

Examine physiological, morphological, and cultural characteristics, using a microscope, to identify and classify microorganisms in human, water, and food specimens. Investigate the relationship between organisms and disease, including the control of epidemics and the effects of antibiotics on microorganisms. Collect and analyze biological data about relationships among and between organisms and their environment. Study growth, structure, development, and general characteristics of bacteria and other microorganisms to understand their relationship to human, plant, and animal health. Investigate diseases or parasites to determine cause and risk factors, progress, life cycle, or mode of transmission.

Prepare plans for management of renewable resources, and develop pest management and control measures using scientific methods. Research and review regulatory, technical, or market issues related to sustainability. Plan or supervise waste management programs for composting or farming. Provide advice regarding the development of regulatory standards for land reclamation or soil conservation. Develop reports or presentations to communicate the effectiveness of sustainability initiatives. Assess or propose sustainability initiatives, considering factors such as cost effectiveness, technical feasibility, and acceptance.

Supervise biological technicians and technologists and other scientists. Provide laboratory services for health departments, community environmental health programs, and physicians needing information for diagnosis and treatment. Communicate test results to state and federal representatives and the general public. Prepare requests for proposals or statements of work. Conduct or oversee contingency plan integration and operation. Develop disaster recovery plans for physical locations with critical assets, such as data centers.

Conduct research to develop methodologies, instrumentation, and procedures for medical application, analyzing data and presenting findings. Advise producers about improved products and techniques that could enhance their animal production efforts. Develop improved practices in feeding, housing, sanitation, or parasite and disease control of animals. Study nutritional requirements of animals and nutritive values of animal feed materials. Conduct research concerning animal nutrition, breeding, or management to improve products or processes. Monitor material performance and evaluate its deterioration.

Develop new or improved methods or products for controlling or eliminating weeds, crop diseases, or pests. Conduct experiments to investigate the underlying mechanisms of plant growth and response to the environment. Identify or create new sustainability indicators. Conduct tests of nuclear fuel behavior and cycles or performance of nuclear machinery and equipment to optimize the performance of existing plants. Analyze product failure data and laboratory test results to determine causes of problems and develop solutions. Monitor tapes or digital recordings to identify the source of losses.
Maintain technical project files. Prepare documentation for case reports or court proceedings. Create or administer training and awareness presentations or materials.

Create mechanical design documents for parts, assemblies, or finished products. Write operational instructions to be used in nuclear plant operation or nuclear fuel or waste handling and disposal. Communicate research findings on various types of diseases to health practitioners, policymakers, and the public.

Attend professional meetings, read literature, and participate in training or other educational offerings to keep abreast of new developments and technologies related to disaster recovery and business continuity. Communicate research or project results to other professionals or the public or teach related courses, seminars, or workshops.

Identify opportunities for strategic improvement or mitigation of business interruption and other risks caused by business, regulatory, or industry-specific change initiatives. Analyze corporate intelligence data to identify trends, patterns, or warnings indicating threats to security of people, assets, information, or infrastructure. Review existing disaster recovery, crisis management, or business continuity plans.

Supervise the work of technologists, technicians, and other engineers and scientists. Oversee the work of contractors in accordance with project requirements. Review and revise sustainability proposals or policies. Monitor or track sustainability indicators, such as energy usage, natural resource usage, waste generation, and recycling.

Develop conceptual designs of security systems, establish security policies, conduct security audits, perform risk analyses, train personnel in security procedures, and implement security practices to ensure compliance and mitigate risks.
Design, implement, or establish requirements for security systems, including video surveillance and access control systems, ensuring proper installation and operation.
Prepare and maintain security documentation, including reports summarizing activities, design drawings, procedures, and recommendations for system improvements.
Conduct inspections of facilities and monitor operations to ensure compliance with applicable health, safety, or hygiene standards, addressing any violations as necessary.
Manage the training of staff and oversee operational aspects of fitness or recreation facilities to ensure the efficacy and safety of programs and services.
Develop, coordinate, and evaluate fitness and wellness programs or services to enhance employee health and wellness.
Maintain documentation of compliance and training activities and provide necessary reports to management or regulatory agencies.
Lead internal investigations and audits to ensure adherence to compliance procedures and address any identified issues or improvements needed.
Prepare management reports, provide project status updates, and assist with applications for necessary permits and regulatory compliance documentation.
Coordinate the scheduling of staff and recreational activities to maximize efficiency and user engagement.
Recruit, interview, or hire employees and verify staff credentials to ensure proper staffing for compliance and operational needs.
Provide training on compliance topics and organizational policies to enhance understanding and enforcement of standards within the organization.
Advise management on compliance programs and facilitate communication related to compliance issues.
Participate in continuous education to stay updated on industry standards and compliance requirements.
Prepare or assist in preparing regulatory agency applications, reports, or correspondence related to wellness and fitness standards.
Create and maintain databases of customer accounts and compliance records, ensuring accurate tracking and reporting.
Monitor compliance systems and ensure their effectiveness in maintaining safety and quality standards for wellness programs.
Identify and follow up on compliance issues that may require investigation or corrective action.
Gather data for evaluating the effectiveness of various programs and strategies implemented in fitness and wellness initiatives.
Report violations of compliance standards to appropriate regulatory agencies as required.
Direct the preparation and implementation of plans for environmental cleanup or wellness program sustainability.
Conduct qualitative and quantitative assessments for health, environmental, or other compliance-related risks.
Create budgets and financial goals for facilities, ensuring resources are allocated efficiently to meet service demands.
Inspect sites and supervise operations to ensure compliance with health and safety regulations during facility assessments.
Identify and apply for project funding or resources to support wellness programs and facilities.
Write reports or summaries related to program implementation, effectiveness, or compliance operations for management review.

Develop and maintain standard operating procedures or local working practices, and ensure correct use and maintenance of physical security systems, such as closed-circuit television, merchandise tags, and burglar alarms.
Train staff in regulatory policies or procedures, and provide regulatory guidance to departments or development project teams regarding design, development, evaluation, or marketing of products.

Review all regulatory agency submission materials to ensure timeliness, accuracy, comprehensiveness, or compliance with regulatory standards, and ensure that regulatory agency requirements are met in marketing literature or user manuals.

Monitor emerging trends regarding industry regulations to determine potential impacts on organizational processes. Maintain current knowledge of relevant regulations, including proposed and final rules.

Manage activities such as audits, regulatory agency inspections, or product recalls and direct the development or implementation of policies and procedures related to compliance throughout an organization.

Investigate product complaints and prepare documentation and submissions to appropriate regulatory agencies as necessary, and provide responses to regulatory agencies regarding product information or issues.
Analyze retail data to identify current or emerging trends in theft or fraud, and recommend improvements in loss prevention programs, staffing, scheduling, or training.
Organize and oversee fitness or wellness programs, such as information presentations, training in first aid or cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR), and evaluate new software publishing systems.

Develop and maintain partnerships with federal, state, or local law enforcement agencies, and develop relationships with state or federal environmental regulatory agencies to learn about and analyze potential impacts of proposed environmental policy regulations.

Communicate regulatory information to multiple departments and ensure that information is interpreted correctly, and explain rules and regulations of facilities and entertainment attractions to customers.

Track attendance, participation, or performance data related to wellness events, and conduct needs assessments or surveys to determine interest in, or satisfaction with, wellness and fitness programs, events, or services.

Provide recommendations and solutions in crisis situations such as workplace violence, protests, and demonstrations, and respond to customer, public, or media requests for information about wellness programs or services.
Plan programs of events or schedules of activities, and organize and oversee health screenings or other preventive measures.
Keep informed regarding pending industry changes, trends, or best practices, and verify that all regulatory policies and procedures have been documented, implemented, and communicated.
Supervise employees or subcontractors to ensure quality of work or adherence to safety regulations or policies, and oversee the maintenance of wind field equipment or structures.

Monitor compliance to operational, safety, or inventory control procedures, including physical security standards, and develop processes or procedures for operations, including transitioning from construction to commercial operations.

Monitor and review paperwork procedures and systems to prevent error-related shortages, and direct documentation efforts to ensure compliance with domestic and international regulations and standards.

Develop marketing campaigns to promote a healthy lifestyle or participation in fitness or wellness programs, and select or supervise contractors, such as event hosts or health, fitness, and wellness practitioners.
